claim: Speech-to-text (STT) combined with LLM agent routing and text-to-speech (TTS) creates a complete personal AI assistant framework
actionable: Build personal assistants using the PAR framework: speech-to-text capture the prompt, agent routing via LLM to select which agent to run, and text-to-speech to return responses
source_title: MOST Important AGENTIC Application - Speech to Text to AI Agents

claim: The PAR framework (Prompt-Agent-Response) creates a clean loop structure for personal AI assistants
actionable: Structure your agentic workflows as Prompt (user input) -> Agent (route and execute) -> Response (feedback to user), then loop back
source_title: MOST Important AGENTIC Application - Speech to Text to AI Agents

claim: Simple keyword-based LLM routing works well for 80% of personal assistant use cases without needing complex routing
actionable: Implement keyword routing by extracting activation keywords from prompts and mapping them to specific agent functions, then let the LLM select which agent to run
source_title: MOST Important AGENTIC Application - Speech to Text to AI Agents

claim: 10-second audio chunks with speech-to-text can miss words at boundaries, requiring improved buffering strategies
actionable: When implementing continuous speech-to-text, avoid fixed time windows for audio capture; use rolling buffers or VAD (Voice Activity Detection) to capture complete phrases
source_title: MOST Important AGENTIC Application - Speech to Text to AI Agents

claim: Personal AI assistants enable agents to modify their own code and agent routing over time for self-improvement
actionable: Design personal AI assistants with the capability to read, modify, and update their own agent routing configuration and function definitions based on usage patterns
source_title: MOST Important AGENTIC Application - Speech to Text to AI Agents
